title: ternary expression with unpacked array produces bad result

features.name: ternary_with_unpacked_array_operands
features.description: Code should include a ternary operator where both branches are unpacked array variables used in procedural assignment. Verilator mishandles this pattern, generating incorrect pointer arithmetic instead of proper array element selection.

features.name: unpacked_array_in_ternary_indexing
features.description: Code should include indexing of unpacked array members within a ternary expression in procedural code. The correct form requires indexing each array operand separately, not the ternary result.

features.name: unpacked_array_element_in_sequential_block
features.description: Code should include unpacked array ports (e.g., input a[1], output logic b[1]) used in sequential always_ff blocks with non-blocking assignment. Verilator's ternary handling fails when array elements are accessed in this context.
features.snippet: input a[1]; output logic b[1]; always_ff @(posedge clk) b <= en ? a : b;
